subject: History
chapter: Bricks, Beads and Bones: The Harappan Civilisation

concepts.category: One of: 'Most Important Fact', 'NCERT Line', 'Board Favourite', 'Memory Trick', 'Common Mistake', 'Units to remember'
concepts.title: Most Important Fact: Trade and Commerce
concepts.description: The Harappan Civilisation was known for its extensive trade and commerce networks, which extended beyond the Indian subcontinent to places like Mesopotamia and the Indus Valley. They traded goods like cotton, indigo, and beads for metals like copper, bronze, and tin.

concepts.title: Urban Planning
concepts.description: The cities of the Harappan Civilisation, such as Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa, were well-planned with a grid system, drains, and public baths, showcasing the advanced urban planning skills of the Harappans.

concepts.title: Script and Writing System
concepts.description: The Harappans used a script that has not yet been deciphered, and their writing system consisted of over 400 symbols, including logograms and phonetic symbols.

concepts.title: Harappan Cities
concepts.description: Mohenjo-Daro and Harappa are the two most famous cities of the Harappan Civilisation, so use the mnemonic 'MDH' to remember them.

concepts.title: Decline of the Civilisation
concepts.description: Students often mistakenly believe that the Harappan Civilisation declined due to environmental degradation or climate change. However, the actual reason for its decline is still a topic of debate among historians, and it's essential to remember that it was a complex process involving multiple factors.

concepts.title: Harappan Economy
concepts.description: The Harappan economy was based on agriculture, trade, and commerce, and it was characterised by a high degree of specialisation and standardisation. The Harappans used weights and measures like the 'maashaana' and the 'karshapana' to standardise trade.

concepts.title: Water Management
concepts.description: The Harappans were skilled in water management, and they built sophisticated systems of canals, dams, and reservoirs to manage the water supply in their cities.

concepts.title: Copper Bangle
concepts.description: The copper bangle, also known as the 'Harappan bangle', is a distinctive artifact of the Harappan Civilisation, and it's often found in Harappan graves and cities.
